item.title: It’s my cake day and here’s the 1st thing I favorited (as is customary) and I can even add a story!
item.description: Funnily enough, I have such a story myself. I’m German and lived in England for 9months in 2004/5 as a foreign language assistant. I’d gone out for a walk (the English sure love their walks :) ), had sat on a bench, when along came this elderly gentleman with his dog. He bade me good day, I replied politely, and he obviously recognized my accent. It immediately threw him back 60yrs; he told me how he’d stayed in Germany as an occupation soldier, how he’d go out with German girls, he said „would you like to dance with me?“ in German, which was so darn cute, and sang part of a German folk song; but he also mentioned how he’d lain in the trenches and cursed every single German man, woman and child. It was a deeply emotional encounter; Imagine going out with German girls in 1945 after fighting a war against their people, then meeting a German girl the same age 60yrs later… I like to imagine that our encounter gave him some kind of closure on what he went through as a young man. He’s surely dead by now and I’m almost 40 with two kids. Life sure is strange, guys… Viral edit: I’m so happy these stories touch so many others! I’ve been reading every single story in the comments, which must have been an major pain to write, what with the comment limitations. And I love all of them! Every time I saw the story of the lady walking from Burma, I thought, one of these days I’m going to tell my story too. Thanks everyone for appreciating it!
item.dc:creator: Fortherea

item.title: Sad day for a lot of American schools
item.description: Awareness is not indoctrination Edit: most viral! Glad so many of you feel the same way. I probably should add that the most shocking ban was announced today: a graphic novel called “Maus” by Art Spiegelman that depicts the Holocaust—apparently it encourages “snitching on your countrymen?” Talk about some BS. Extra Sauce: https://abcnews.go.com/amp/US/pulitzer-prize-winning-holocaust-book-maus-joins-list/story?id=82508346
item.dc:creator: boobsoffury

item.title: No words
item.description: For context, I’m a nurse. My husband has been battling prostate cancer for the last year. Finally, after multiple biopsies, tests, scans, and major surgery his PSA is undetectable which means he is cancer free! Gotta say, kinda don’t appreciate my manager calling to scream at me at 6am because I can’t come in to pick up a shift, and uses my time off work to take care of my husband as a “gotcha”. And people want to know why nurses are leaving the profession in droves. Time to find a new place of employment. #cancersucks #fuckthat
item.dc:creator: nursenurseynurse7

item.title: Work of Auschwitz Survivor & Artist Jan Komsky (Warning: Disturbing images)
item.description: Komsky was a member of the Polish Underground who was on the first train to Auschwitz, and was Prisoner number 564. On December 29, 1942, he escaped from Auschwitz with several other members of the underground and hid out in Broszkowice, in the same building as a senior SS Officer. He then went to Cracow where, just 16 days after escaping, he was caught and arrested, (under a different name) and was sent back to Auschwitz (under a different name). He survived Auschwitz, and post war in the DP Camps he used his time to draw and paint dozens of pieces of what he witnessed with his own eyes. Remember these Images, this is what happened. When you make Nazi Comparisons, think of these and think, it this remotely similar. To learn more, watch the documentary Eyewitness (1999) about his life and work.
item.dc:creator: Hirschtory

item.title: Being a girl is fun
item.description: I’ve worked in the industry for 10 years now. I have a think skin, I’m used to bar managers thinking I don’t know what I am talking about. I’ve been called “little lady” and “sweetheart” more times than I can count, but having people who do my same job and who I thought were my friends call me “slutty” breaks my fucking heart. I love my job and people like that are the reason I go sit in my car and cry. Sorry for the rant. I’m just hurt.
item.dc:creator: elveswithattitude

item.title: Auschwitz: Understanding what it truly was
item.description:
Picture Credit: Me
Picture Credit: Me
Picture Credit: Me
Picture Credit: Me
the man who designed the furnaces to burn the bodies did so on his free time as a project to please Hitler.
Actual Blue Prints
Actual Blue Prints
Picture Credit: Me I hope this gave a brief introduction to the horrors of Auschwitz on the anniversary of its liberation. For more about this, please look up the writings of my friend and associate Robert Jan van Pelt. The foremost expert on Auschwitz today.

item.title: Holocaust Memorial Day - The Roma
item.description:
Eva Justin was born on 23 August 1909 in Dresden. She graduated from High School in 1933, the same year Hitler became Chancellor of Germany, and began to study to become a nurse at the University Hospital in Tübingen under Dr Robert Ritter. Robert Ritter (b. 14 May 1901) was at Tübingen completing studies in child psychology and “criminal inheritability” – the idea that people might, in modern understanding, have a genetic predisposition to ‘criminal behaviour’. In the 1930s, this field was heavily entwined with the eugenics and ‘racial purity’ pseudo-scientific political movements. In 1936 Ritter was appointed head of the Racial Hygiene and Demographic Biology Research Unit, a division of the German Kriminalpolizei (Kripo).
[Picture: Robert Ritter interviewing in 1936; note he is accompanied by Police] Ritter took Eva Justin with him to this new role as his deputy, the pair were put in charge of a project from the Reich Security Main Office (RSHA – headed by SS leader Heinrich Himmler) to do a national survey of Germany’s Roma population. Ritter’s studies of, and belief in, hereditary criminality were linked to a long pan-European tradition of perceiving Roma peoples as habitual criminal elements. As early as 1899 the Munich Police had a “Gypsy Intelligence” team who specifically monitored the group, in 1927, the state of Prussia began attempting to force Roma moving through the state to submit to mandatory “preventative” fingerprinting and photographing for Police records. In 1936 such state systems formed the basis for a new Reich Central Office for Combating Gypsy Crime. Ritter’s influence on these anti-Roma organisations began early, he had ‘identified’ a condition by which, he claimed, those with a hereditary criminal disposition could appear ‘normal’ – law-abiding, rational, etc – under investigation. This effectively allowed a person perceived as criminal due to social, political, or racial bias to be condemned at any time; an unknown number of Roma people were sterilised based on this nonsense diagnosis.
[Picture: Eva Justin interviewing, 1936] Robert Ritter and Eva Justin threw themselves into the nationwide survey of the estimated 30,000 Roma in Germany, interviewing individuals about their family background, their relatives, and movements. Simultaneously, local record offices were examined for information about Roma movements. Ritter’s pseudo-scientific approach was aided by Justin, who took blood samples, measured the sizes of heads, the distance between the eyes, and other such biological data. Justin learnt to speak several Roma dialects, and was able to elicit deeper and more wide-ranging response from those interviewed. However, where individuals were uncooperative, the pair retained the ability to arrest and confine in concentration camps. Ritter established that Roma were “Aryans”, but that as much as 90% of Germany’s Roma population was ‘unacceptably’ racially intermixed. He concluded Roma were both a dangerous ‘hereditary’ criminal element to society, and a degenerate ‘racial purity’ threat to the future of the German race. He personally recommended mass sterilisation as a ‘humane’ next step.
[Picture: Eva Julian measuring the head of a Roma boy at Kripo offices in Stuttgart, April 1938). This accorded smoothly with centuries of anti-Roma preconceptions and Nazi racial ideology and became the “scientific” basis for Nazi action against Roma. While the Nazi concentration camp system of the mid-1930s was primarily still a prison system, albeit a spectacularly harsh one, for Roma ghettoization into concentration camps based on their ethnicity began in 1935 with the creation of euphemistically named “Gypsy Rest Areas”. These were defined plots of land where local Roma people would be forcibly settled by local authorities.
[Picture: A Police raid on a Roma encampment, Renningen, winter 1937/38.] The first of these was at Cologne-Bickendorf (known as “Black and White Square”), and was followed by other ‘rest areas’ across Germany, such as Hannover (Altwarmbüchener Moor), Magdeburg Holzweg, Kiel (for the grand total of 24 Roma there), Ravensburg, and Salzburg-Maxglan. In Berlin, a camp opened at Marzahn in July 1936, allowing the Berlin authorities to round up and confine Berlin’s Roma population – 600 people - before the beginning of the 1936 Berlin Olympics on 1st August. A Nazi press release announced the confinement was to “protect neighbourly coexistence”.
[Picture: Police assigned to guard the Berlin ‘rest area’, 1936/37.] These rest areas were prison camps in all but name, some were encircled with wire fences and had guarded entry and exit points, with signing in and out of individuals, and curfews. All were subject to heavy Police supervision. They were subject to constant intrusive ‘scientific’ research from pseudo-scientists like Gerhart Stein and Otmar von Verschuer (the man who gave Josef Mengele his PhD). The separation and removal of these people from the Nazi “Volkgemeinschaft” (People’s Community, the idealised national socialist Germany Nazism was trying to build) was considered essential, although some, including Ritter, paternalistically imagined that the Nazi-defined tiny number of so-called “pure gypsies” could continue to exist in a kind of “reservation”.
[Picture: Roma in Belzec concentration camp.] This distinction between “pure” and “degenerate” Roma did not survive contact with the wartime Nazi regime. From 1940, in the chaotic internal politics of Nazi Germany, disagreement between different factions in the one-party state about the Roma led initially to mixed outcomes; some Roma continued to live in their camps, while others were deported to the rapidly-expanding concentration camp system as slave labour, while others were massacred en masse by Einsatzgruppen. A final direction of travel came on 16 December 1942, when Himmler ordered that Roma already designated as having no place in the Volksgemeinschaft were to be deported to the dedicated killing centre at Auschwitz, this was followed on 15 November 1943 with a directive that Roma were to be put “on the same level as Jews”.
[Picture: Roma being rounded up by Orpo in Asperg 1940.] At this time, Robert Ritter and Eva Justin formed together one of the foremost Nazi authorities on the Roma, and to this end, Ritter supported Justin in her attempt to earn a PhD. This took the form of a study of 39 Roma children – 20 girls and 19 boys – who were held in an orphanage in Mulfingen after being abducted from their parents. The study was to ascertain if Roma children could be nurtured out of the ‘hereditary criminal’ influence of their “primitive nomad” families. In her foreward to her dissertation, after thanking Dr Ritter, she said she hoped his & her work will ‘direct future racial hygiene legislation, and further reduce the influence of inferior, primitive, genetic material on the German people.’ She concluded that, based on her observation of the children, that all Roma children should be sterilised, while German-Roma ‘mixed’ children should be sterilised at the first sight of any anti-social behaviour. The 39 children had, for the duration of her PhD study, been exempt from the actual policies of the regime Justin served; with her PhD awarded in March 1944, the 39 “research subjects” were deported on 9 May 1944 to Auschwitz.
[Picture: 8 of the 39 Roma children studied by Eva Justin, pictures taken by her of her 'subjects'.] Roma were usually classified in the camp system as “asocials” (black triangles) or “criminals” (green triangles), sometimes marked with a Z. Unusually, Roma families were imprisoned together in the “Gypsy Family Camp” at Auschwitz; this facilitated their "study" by Dr Josef Mengele. This camp closed in August 1944, with the "healthy adult" population transferred to forced labour elsewhere in the complex of Auschwitz camps, the rest were murdered. According to a few survivors, some of the Roma put up a final doomed defence, fighting the SS to the death with shovels, planks of wood, and pipes. Of 23,000 Roma sent to Auschwitz, 19,000 were murdered. This included 35 of the 39 children studied by Eva Justin.
[Picture: Roma being marched from Asperg, May 1940, they would eventually reach Nazi-occupied Poland 800km (500 miles) away and be ‘distributed’ to various Ghettos.] After World War Two, Dr Robert Ritter and Dr Eva Justin both successfully escaped prosecution for providing pseudo-scientific justification to the Nazi regime. Ritter was tracked down and taken to court by Vincent Rose, a Roma survivor of Natzweiler-Struthof concentration camp in 1949. Ritter successfully defended himself by suggesting Mr Rose, and other Roma witnesses, were “anti-social liars” seeking revenge on him for his “well founded” scientific research. He was acquitted, and worked as a paediatrician in Frankfurt until his death in 1951, he re-employed Dr Eva Justin as a psychologist specialising in children. In 1958 Justin was herself investigated, but was cleared as “not knowing” what the consequences of her research would be, her instruction to sterilise Roma children was ruled outside the statute of limitations. She continued working as a “Roma expert” in Frankfurt until her death from cancer in 1964. It was only in October 2001 that the last German Police service known to record the identities of Roma in their locality for “crime prevention” reasons ceased to do so.
[Video: The children, Eva Justin is behind the camera.] The children of Mulfingen who were murdered on 2 August 1944: Rudi Delis, Maria Delis, Rudolf Eckstein, Fritz Eckstein, Martin (Markus) Eckstein, Amandus Eckstein, Patrizka Georges, Wilhelm Georges, Sofie Georges, Ferdinand Georges, Johanna Köhler, Franz Köhler, Anton Köhler, Olga Köhler, Anton Köhler, Elise Köhler, Johann Köhler, Josef Köhler, Sonja Kurz, Otto Kurz, Thomas Kurz, Martha Mai, Sofie Mai, Karl Mai, Elisabeth Mai, Johanna Reinhard, Klara Reinhard, Scholastika Reinhard, Adolf Reinhard, Siegfried Schneck, Luana Schneck, Karl Weiss, Maria Winter, Rosina Winter, and Josef Winter. The survivors were: Amalie Reinhard, Luise May, Rosa Georges, and Andreas Reinhardt. As they were all older-looking, they were selected for slave labour. Amalie Reinhard (Z-10635) spoke publicly about her experience for the first time in 1994. The last words her youngest sister said to her were: "You’re going away and we’re going to be burned.” It is estimated that half to three-quarters of all European Roma died in the Holocaust, taking in anywhere from 500,000 to 1.5 million people; the Roma were the target and victim of a marriage of bigotry between scientific racism and social discrimination, many of the tenets of which remain common in non-Roma discourse about “what to do about” Roma today. The Roma remember the Holocaust’s effect on their people as the Porajmos, “The Devouring.” --------- Other victims of the Holocaust: Black People: https://imgur.com/gallery/06ykonO LGBT people: https://imgur.com/gallery/xIbrOJW Mentally ill & disabled people: https://imgur.com/gallery/fb4IEAk Photographing the victims: https://imgur.com/gallery/YKywOzU Heroes, villains, and both: Kastner: https://imgur.com/gallery/yxsDfew Sugihara: https://imgur.com/gallery/vdW86Xb Perl: https://imgur.com/gallery/je5JkQd Bruno & Otto: https://imgur.com/gallery/t9d4ToO Rumkowski: https://imgur.com/gallery/sG3ywZF Coward: https://imgur.com/gallery/hHVzpIu Gerstein: https://imgur.com/gallery/pNQepwe
item.dc:creator: BodicaRexVII

item.title: My sister modeled as Harley Quinn and made the cover of Batman detective
item.description:
#dc_comics #dc #batman
Our friend Irvin Rodriguez is a very talented painter who started working for DC and used his friends and family as models. She made the cover. Getting it framed for her this week! Irvin’s work can be found on instagram here- https://instagram.com/irvinrodriguez
item.dc:creator: soundscape462

item.title: That was a first!
item.description: Northern California resident. Small mom/pop Indian place. Hole in a wall, at a nearly deserted strip mall. Very polite, and apologetic, he told me if I’m not vaccinated I can order for take out, but in order to dine in, I had to be vaccinated. I really don’t understand why is it a big deal. Also, that was the first time someone asked me for proof of vaccination since the whole shitstorm started.
item.dc:creator: NotSteveWozniak

item.title: Dear Tennessee: Fuck You
item.description: Come and fucking take them from me you cowardly fucks. You can try to erase history and hide from the truth, but I for one will continue to share these words and images everywhere I can. https://www.cp24.com/world/holocaust-novel-maus-banned-in-tennessee-school-district-1.5757109
item.dc:creator: HomeDepotAisle15
